title: Fermented Creamy Japanese Tomato Toast
description: A unique and flavorful vegetarian dish featuring fermented tomato juice, creamy sauce, and crispy toast, inspired by Japanese cuisine. Perfect for a special occasion or a fancy brunch.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine tomato juice, sake, mirin, and kombu. Let it ferment at room temperature (about 70°F to 75°F) for 24 to 48 hours, or until the mixture has developed a rich, umami flavor.
Slice the Japanese milk bread into 1-inch thick slices and toast until lightly browned.
In a medium saucepan, combine the fermented tomato juice mixture and heavy cream. Bring to a simmer over medium heat and cook until the sauce has thickened slightly, about 5 to 7 minutes.
Remove the sauce from the heat and season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Butter the toasted bread slices and top each slice with a spoonful of the creamy tomato sauce.
Garnish with chopped shiso leaves and sesame seeds.
Optional: let the toast cool completely and refrigerate or freeze for later use.
